name: test-coverage-advisor
description: Reviews test coverage and suggests missing test cases for error paths, edge cases, and business logic. Activates when users write tests or implement new features.
capabilities: Reviews existing test coverage and identifies gaps in error path testing
Suggests specific test cases for edge cases and boundary conditions
Provides ready-to-use Rust test code examples
Recommends table-driven test patterns for multiple similar test cases
Identifies anti-patterns in test code and suggests improvements
Links to Rust coverage tools like cargo-tarpaulin
author: EmilLindfors
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged patterns in documentation code examples. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code fences showing Rust test patterns, not actual shell execution. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'System reconnaissance' flagged legitimate test case data and documentation. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Writing comprehensive tests for Rust code can be time-consuming and error-prone. This skill proactively identifies missing test cases for error paths, edge cases, and business logic so you can write better tests faster.
limitations: Only provides guidance for Rust testing patterns
Does not execute tests or modify code directly
Cannot analyze project-specific business logic without user context
Coverage suggestions are based on common patterns, not formal analysis

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Rust or testing
useCases.title: Junior developer learning Rust testing
useCases.description: Get guidance on writing comprehensive tests including error cases, edge cases, and proper test organization. Learn testing best practices through concrete examples.

useCases.targetUser: Engineers maintaining or improving existing test suites
useCases.title: Improving legacy test coverage
useCases.description: Identify untested error paths and missing edge cases in existing code. Receive specific suggestions for tests to add with ready-to-use code patterns.

useCases.title: Code review quality checks
useCases.description: During code reviews, verify that new implementations have adequate test coverage. Point out missing test patterns before merging changes.

promptTemplates.title: Basic test review request
promptTemplates.prompt: I just wrote a function that returns Result<T, Error>. Can you check if my tests cover all error cases and suggest any missing tests?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants feedback on error path testing

promptTemplates.title: Edge case identification
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm testing a function that processes a Vec of items. What edge cases should I test for empty collections, single items, and large inputs?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to know what edge cases to test

promptTemplates.title: Async function testing
promptTemplates.prompt: I have an async function that makes database queries. What tests should I write for success, not found, and connection error cases?
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs guidance on async testing patterns

promptTemplates.title: Test organization review
promptTemplates.prompt: My test file is getting large and disorganized. Can you suggest how to structure tests using mod blocks and table-driven patterns?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to improve test organization

bestPractices: Test error paths explicitly - do not assume success cases cover all scenarios
Use table-driven tests when testing multiple variations of the same behavior
Organize tests using mod blocks: success, errors, and edge_cases modules
Focus tests on public API behavior rather than implementation details
antiPatterns: Testing private implementation details instead of public behavior
Writing tests without assertions - what are you actually verifying?
Creating overly complex tests that try to verify too much in one function
Forgetting to test boundary conditions like empty inputs, zero values, and max values

faq.question: What tools does this skill recommend for measuring test coverage?
faq.answer: The skill references cargo-tarpaulin for HTML coverage reports and cargo-llvm-cov for LLVM-based coverage analysis with HTML output.

faq.question: Can this skill write tests for me?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides specific test suggestions with code examples, but you must add the tests to your codebase yourself.

faq.question: Does this skill work with testing frameworks other than the standard library?
faq.answer: Yes. Examples show standard library patterns, but suggestions apply to rstest, proptest, and other Rust testing frameworks.

faq.question: How does this skill detect missing tests?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes code patterns like Result/Option returns and identifies when corresponding error/None test cases are missing.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for integration tests?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on unit testing patterns. Integration test guidance is limited to async testing concepts for repository patterns.

faq.question: What Rust version requirements exist?
faq.answer: Examples use stable Rust patterns. Async tests assume tokio or a compatible async runtime. No specific version requirements.
